root_cause_analysis.symptom: Prior cycle 20260519-175339 self-identified 10 shortcomings during /close debate; 6 were classified SYSTEMIC infra defects, 3 NEED-TO-FIX behavioral disciplines, 1 ACCEPTABLE-DEBT.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Cumulative infra debt: (a) tool-policy contract inconsistency with agent.md codex_required mandates (item 7 — recurs every cycle); (b) pattern-match grant mechanism is trivially spoofable + bypassable (item 2); (c) push-grant Chain-B short-circuit relies on orchestrator chaining semantics (item 1); (d) commit-grant TTL structurally too short for subagent latency (item 4); (e) /commit Step 7 silent skip is fail-open data contract (item 5); (f) QA test harness has no trap-on-exit (item 10); (g) discipline reminders missing in close.md/dev.md/qa.md/commit.md (items 3, 6, 9).
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A (retrospective items span multiple commits and infra design decisions; no single commit caused all 10)
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Each item has its own genealogy; retrospective JSON items[].final_rationale fields document each one. Tool-policy gaps were original 2026-05-09 omissions; grant-pattern matching was original 2026-04 design; push-analyst TTL was 60s and bumped to 120s in a prior cycle but is still too tight.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Recurrence — every cycle hits at least some of these gaps. This BA session hit item 7 live during codex consult (codex tee blocked).
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Items accumulated across multiple cycles; the prior cycle 20260519-175339 was the first to formally enumerate them in a structured retrospective. The retrospective was produced 2026-05-20T05:05:00Z; this BA spec follows at 2026-05-20T05:30:00Z.

development_approach.strategy: Implement 9 items in retrospective priority order (7 > 2 > 1 > 5 > 4 > 10 > 6 > 3 > 9). R7 first because every subsequent cycle hits the gap; R2 second because grant-mechanism redesign is the deepest change; R1 third because push-grant integrity needs single-process semantics before R4's TTL bump matters. R3/R6/R9 are 1-line doc additions, lowest risk, last.
development_approach.validation_approach: QA runs the file-content-grep recipes declared in each AC of docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md. No long-lived child process spawning (per R10 spirit). For R7, QA additionally runs jq queries against policies/tool-policy.v1.json to verify Skill in 9 role entries + policy_version=3.

findings.issue: R1 routes through wrong file (scripts/write-commit-grant.py is commit-grant, not push-grant)
findings.proposed_fix: Remove scripts/write-commit-grant.py from R1; route through hooks/push.sh + commands/push.md sentinel/exec patterns
findings.rationale: scripts/write-commit-grant.py uses GRANT_TTL_MINUTES=10 for COMMIT grants; push-analyst Chain-B is a separate mechanism.

findings.issue: R10 under-scopes ACs (named AC1/AC2/AC11 but target spec has AC1/AC2/AC3/AC4/AC11)
findings.proposed_fix: Expand R10 to all 5 ACs in target spec
findings.rationale: Read of spec-20260520-044700.md confirms 5 ACs spawn long-lived child processes.

findings.issue: R5 'cycle window' is underspecified, can choose wrong/multiple specs or still silently pass
findings.proposed_fix: Define cycle-window source explicitly; context first, then glob; one match = fail with 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context'; multiple = fail requiring explicit linkage
findings.rationale: Deterministic fail-closed semantics required to prevent the silent-skip recurrence.

findings.issue: R4 target file question (write-commit-grant.py vs push-analyst.md)
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed agents/push-analyst.md is the right target; scripts/write-commit-grant.py is the wrong mechanism. Document TTL in commands/push.md.

Codex confirmed item 8 acceptable-debt OOS is correct.
Codex confirmed priority is informational for sequencing, not AC numbering.

iter3_objections.claim: Context-20260519-211515.json is missing the top-level `iteration_log[]` field that the dispatch prompt explicitly required to contain an iter2 entry. Direct grep of the file returns no top-level `iteration_log` key. BA's iter3 narrative talks about the iter1/iter2 history in the collision_report_recurring section and in iter3_objections_resolved, but does not produce the structured iteration_log[] array.
iter3_objections.problem: Downstream auditors and the close-cycle report inspector expect a structured iteration_log[] entry per BA-QA iteration so the cycle's revision history is queryable. The current context is missing this entirely. The dispatch prompt for iter3 explicitly listed 'iteration_log[] has iter2 entry (the iter that just landed; counting from iter1 as the first revision)' as part of OBJ-6 resolution criteria.
iter3_objections.required_evidence: Orchestrator MUST add an iteration_log[] array to context-20260519-211515.json with at least an iter2 entry (the BA iter that landed before iter3 began), including iter1 if backfill is desired. Example shape: [{'iter': 1, 'resolved': ['OBJ-1'], 'introduced': ['OBJ-4', 'OBJ-5', 'OBJ-6']}, {'iter': 2, 'resolved': [], 'introduced': []}].

iter3_objections.claim: Context-20260519-211515.json `collision_report_recurring.second_collision.preserved_paths` (lines 104-106) lists `docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515-DH.md` and `docs/dev/context-20260519-211515-DH.json`. Direct `ls -la /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/docs/dev/` confirms neither file exists on disk. On-disk reality: only `-CYCLE2` and `-CYCLE2-round2` ghost files exist; D+H content has been promoted by the orchestrator to the canonical `-211515.md` and `-211515.json` paths.
iter3_objections.problem: The preserved_paths field is misleading. Any downstream auditor following these paths will fail to find the artifacts, and may falsely conclude the D+H content was lost. The dispatch prompt for iter3 explicitly required `collision_report.preserved_artifact_paths[] reflects on-disk state (-CYCLE2 suffix, not -DH)`.
iter3_objections.required_evidence: Orchestrator MUST update collision_report_recurring.second_collision.preserved_paths to either (a) reference the actual on-disk -CYCLE2 ghost paths (ticket-20260519-211515-CYCLE2.md, ticket-20260519-211515-CYCLE2-round2.md, context-20260519-211515-CYCLE2.json, context-20260519-211515-CYCLE2-round2.json) which are the PARALLEL cycle's artifacts, OR (b) note that the second collision was resolved by orchestrator-promoting D+H content to canonical slots (no -DH artifacts persist), with the parallel cycle's content quarantined under -CYCLE2 suffix.

iter3_objections.claim: Context-20260519-211515.json `complexity_tier: "STANDARD"` (line 7) + `acceptance_criteria_path: null` (line 55) violates the /dev Step 8 test-writer dispatch contract. Direct read of commands/dev.md confirms: line 606-610 dispatches test-writer when tier ∈ {STANDARD, COMPLEX} OR risk_level == high; lines 620-623 specify the dispatch prompt passes `acceptance_criteria_path: docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-<task_id>.json`; agents/test-writer.md lines 24-29 and 178 require this path to be non-null. BA's `_companion_artifacts_note` (line 57) acknowledges this gap but does not resolve it.
iter3_objections.problem: Downstream Dev dispatch WILL fail or load stale Cycle 2 content. If orchestrator dispatches test-writer with null path, test-writer aborts. If orchestrator uses the existing on-disk file at docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json, that file contains Cycle 2 ACs (verified per iter2 QA report OBJ-6 evidence), not D+H ACs, so test-writer generates tests for the wrong scope.
iter3_objections.required_evidence: Orchestrator MUST choose between: (R1) overwrite docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json with D+H ACs derived from the 10 ACs in the ticket (AC-D1, AC-D2, AC-D3, AC-D4, AC-H1, AC-H2, AC-H3a, AC-H3b, AC-H4, AC-D-H), and update context line 55 to point to it; OR (R2) downgrade context complexity_tier to SMALL, which bypasses the test-writer dispatch gate per commands/dev.md:608 (SMALL/MICRO tiers do not trigger test_writer_expected=true).

ba_qa_unresolved_objections.iter3_summary: iter3 FINAL: OBJ-4 and OBJ-5 cleanly resolved (BLOCKER-level dimension fixes complete). OBJ-6 remains UNRESOLVED with two concrete defects in the canonical context JSON: (a) NO `iteration_log[]` field exists anywhere in context-20260519-211515.json — the dispatch prompt explicitly required `iteration_log[] has iter2 entry`, and this field is entirely absent. (b) `collision_report_recurring.second_collision.preserved_paths` (lines 104-106) still references `-DH` suffixed paths (docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515-DH.md, docs/dev/context-20260519-211515-DH.json) that DO NOT EXIST on disk — on-disk state shows only `-CYCLE2` and `-CYCLE2-round2` suffixed ghost files, with D+H content promoted to the canonical 20260519-211515 slots. The dispatch prompt explicitly required `collision_report.preserved_artifact_paths[] reflects on-disk state (-CYCLE2 suffix, not -DH)`. BA failed to update both fields. Additionally, Codex's prior ISSUE-1 (test-writer contract gap) was verified by direct read of commands/dev.md:606-630 and agents/test-writer.md — the gap is REAL, not forward-looking: STANDARD tier dispatches test-writer expecting non-null acceptance_criteria_path, but BA set it to null per dispatch instruction. Codex iter3 adversarial review independently arrived at FAIL with the same evidence. Per dev.md max-3 BA-QA loop ceiling, this is the final iteration; orchestrator will proceed with best-effort BA output but MUST resolve OBJ-6 metadata defects and Codex ISSUE-1 contract gap before Dev dispatch (either regenerate companion AC JSON from the 10 D+H ACs, OR downgrade complexity_tier to SMALL to bypass test-writer dispatch, AND patch the iteration_log + preserved_paths fields).
